Description:
Replace in-memory fake DB with Supabase PostgreSQL + pgvector, configure asyncpg connection pool, set up Alembic migrations, and define full domain models.

SCOPE: MVP 2
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Database
STORIES: 8.1 - 8.4

Migrate all data storage from in-memory dict to Supabase PostgreSQL with pgvector extension.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Enable pgvector extension in Supabase project
- Remove fake_db dict and helper functions from main.py
- Update all agent run() methods to insert via asyncpg instead of fake_db
- Update jobs router to query PostgreSQL instead of fake_db
- Enable Row-Level Security on all tables (configured in MVP 3 Story 14.2)
- Update .env.example with SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, DATABASE_URL
- Stub RLS policies (permissive for MVP 2, locked down in MVP 3)

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Database
DEPENDENCIES: JD-18, JD-44 (Alembic migrations)

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Jobs persisted across server restarts
- GET /api/v1/jobs queries PostgreSQL
- pgvector extension installed and active
- No references to fake_db remain in codebase

EDGE CASES:
- Migration from fake_db data not required (dev data only)
- Supabase connection limits: pool tuned to stay within free tier limits

Create backend/db.py with SQLAlchemy 2 async engine and explicitly tuned asyncpg connection pool.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- create_async_engine with: pool_size=10, max_overflow=20, pool_timeout=30, pool_recycle=1800, pool_pre_ping=True
- URL from settings.database_url (asyncpg dialect)
- Export async_session_maker using AsyncSession
- Add get_db() async generator for FastAPI dependency injection
- Log pool creation event via get_logger
- Comment: pool_size tuned to uvicorn --workers 2 (each worker gets own pool)

EFFORT: M (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-14

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Engine created without errors on startup
- pool_pre_ping catches stale connections
- get_db() yields session and closes on exit
- No connection leaks under concurrent requests

EDGE CASES:
- Supabase connection string uses postgresql+asyncpg:// scheme
- pool_recycle=1800 prevents idle connection timeout from Supabase proxy

Set up Alembic in backend/migrations/ with auto-migration on container startup via Supervisor.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Run: uv run alembic init backend/migrations
- Configure env.py to use settings.database_url from settings.py
- Create initial migration: jobs table with all Job model fields + embedding vector(1536) column
- Add migration for: recruiter, application, cv, cover_letter, scrape_run, interview_prep tables (Story 8.4)
- Verify supervisord migrate program runs alembic upgrade head at priority=1
- Add alembic downgrade -1 to admin process documentation

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Database
DEPENDENCIES: JD-41

TESTING CRITERIA:
- alembic upgrade head runs without errors
- All tables created with correct schema
- Rollback via alembic downgrade -1 works
- Supervisor runs migrate before uvicorn

EDGE CASES:
- concurrent migration runs must be prevented (alembic uses advisory lock)
- Add alembic_version table check to health endpoint

Expand backend/models.py with all MVP 2 domain models: Job, Recruiter, Application, CV, CoverLetter, ScrapeRun, InterviewPrep.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Job: add embedding: list[float] | None field for pgvector, add recruiter_id FK
- Recruiter: id, name, company, linkedin_url, email, quality_score (float), scraped_at
- Application: id, job_id (FK), status (enum: draft/submitted/interviewing/rejected/offer), applied_at, notes
- CV: id, content (str), embedding: list[float], version (int), created_at
- CoverLetter: id, job_id (FK), content (str), ats_keyword_match (float), created_at
- ScrapeRun: id, source_id, jobs_found (int), jobs_inserted (int), duration_seconds (float), run_at
- InterviewPrep: id, job_id (FK), questions (list[str]), system_design_topics (list[str]), salary_benchmark (str), created_at
- All models: UUID primary keys, created_at datetime, Pydantic v2 syntax

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All models import without errors
- Pydantic v2 validation correct for all field types
- Alembic migration generated correctly from models

EDGE CASES:
- vector(1536) type requires pgvector SQLAlchemy extension type
- Enum fields need SQLAlchemy Enum type plus Pydantic literal type

Build the AI ranking pipeline, RAG personalisation agent, cover letter agent, and question-answer agent with Ragas eval in CI.

SCOPE: MVP 2
PROJECT AREA: Backend / AI Agents
STORIES: 9.1 - 9.5

Build backend/agents/ranking/ranking_agent.py with 8-step AI relevance scoring pipeline.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Step 1: Generate job embedding using BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 via LiteLLM
- Step 2: Cosine similarity against CV embedding (pgvector <-> operator)
- Step 3: Cross-encoder rerank using cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2
- Step 4: Sentiment analysis on recruiter message tone (positive/neutral/negative)
- Step 5: Recruiter quality score (from Recruiter.quality_score field)
- Step 6: Salary normalisation (extract and normalise salary range from description)
- Step 7: Skill extraction (LLM call — extract required skills list)
- Step 8: Seniority validation (confirm role is senior/lead/principal/architect level)
- Store similarity_score on Job record; threshold >= 0.75 for insertion
- Run via Temporal workflow (async execution)

EFFORT: XL (12 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / AI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-43, JD-41

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 8 steps execute in sequence
- Embedding stored in pgvector job.embedding field
- Jobs below threshold not surfaced in dashboard
- Ranking results deterministic (temperature=0)

EDGE CASES:
- Missing CV embedding: skip steps 2-3, score based on keyword match only
- LLM API timeout: use cached embedding if available

Build backend/agents/rag/rag_agent.py with contextual retrieval from CV, applications, and recruiter data.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Retrieval sources: CV (latest version), application history, recruiter messages, saved jobs, user preferences, skill graph
- Embedding-based retrieval using pgvector similarity search
- Context window budget: 8000 tokens max for retrieval context
- Chunk CV into 512-token segments with 50-token overlap
- Personalise job recommendations using retrieved context
- Expose via POST /api/v1/personalise/{job_id} endpoint
- Eval metrics: Ragas retrieval precision >= 0.80, context recall >= 0.75

EFFORT: XL (12 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / AI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-44, JD-43

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Retrieval returns relevant CV sections for given job
- Context window budget respected
- Ragas metrics meet thresholds in eval run
- Personalised recommendations differ from non-personalised

EDGE CASES:
- No CV uploaded: return non-personalised results with warning
- Retrieval context too large: truncate with priority order (CV > applications > recruiter messages)

Build backend/agents/cover-letter/cover_letter_agent.py with ATS-optimised generation playbook.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Structured playbook sections: role summary, matching skills, quantified achievements, AI narrative, ATS keywords, recruiter-focused closing
- LLM call (medium reasoning effort, temperature=0) via LiteLLM
- ATS keyword match: extract keywords from job description; measure overlap with generated letter; enforce >= 60% match
- If match < 60%: regenerate with explicit keyword injection instruction (max 2 retries)
- Store CoverLetter record with ats_keyword_match score
- Expose via POST /api/v1/cover-letter/{job_id}

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / AI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-43, JD-45

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Generated letter contains all 6 playbook sections
- ATS keyword match >= 60% enforced
- Letter stored in CoverLetter table
- Retry logic triggers when first draft below threshold

EDGE CASES:
- Job description with no extractable keywords: warn and generate without ATS enforcement
- Letter length: enforce 300-500 words

Build backend/agents/question-answer/question_answer_agent.py for RAG-grounded Q&A on job listings.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- POST /api/v1/question-answer/{job_id} accepts {question: str}
- Retrieve context: job description, company metadata, user CV, recruiter profile
- Ground answer in retrieved context only — no hallucination
- Return {answer: str, sources: list[str], confidence: float}
- High reasoning effort (xhigh) for complex technical questions
- Log token usage per invocation via get_logger

EFFORT: L (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / AI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-45

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Answer grounded in retrieved context
- Sources cited in response
- Unanswerable questions return explicit I don't know response
- Token usage logged

EDGE CASES:
- Question about salary when none in job description: cite market rate sources only
- Very long job descriptions: chunk and retrieve relevant sections

Add Ragas retrieval precision and context recall metrics to CI eval pipeline.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Install ragas as dev dependency
- Add ragas metrics to run_evals.py: ContextPrecision >= 0.80, ContextRecall >= 0.75
- Create evals/rag/eval-set-v1.json with 5 minimum RAG test cases
- Each test case: {question, contexts: list[str], ground_truth_answer}
- Add eval-regression-rag job to ci.yml (push to main only)
- Block deploy if precision < 0.80

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: AI Quality / CI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-45, JD-39

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Ragas metrics computed per eval case
- Threshold enforcement causes CI failure
- eval-set-v1.json has valid structure

EDGE CASES:
- Ragas requires OpenAI API key by default — document local model alternative

Build the security agent for prompt injection defence, workflow orchestrator with Temporal, circuit breakers, and admin DLQ routes.

SCOPE: MVP 2
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Security
STORIES: 10.1 - 10.4

Build backend/agents/security/security_agent.py with comprehensive input validation and prompt injection defence.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Prompt injection detection: scan all LLM inputs for injection patterns (ignore previous instructions, system prompt override, jailbreak patterns)
- OWASP middleware: FastAPI middleware validating all request bodies against Pydantic schemas before routing
- Schema validation: reject any request with unexpected fields (strict Pydantic mode)
- RBAC enforcement: validate JWT claims on protected routes (admin-only endpoints)
- HTML sanitisation: strip all HTML tags from user-supplied text fields using bleach
- Audit logging: log all security events (injection attempt, schema violation, RBAC denial) as structured JSON

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Security
DEPENDENCIES: JD-14, JD-15

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Injection patterns detected and rejected with 400 response
- OWASP middleware blocks malformed requests
- Admin routes reject unauthenticated requests
- All security events appear in structured logs

EDGE CASES:
- False positive injection detection must not block legitimate queries — tune regex patterns
- bleach must allowlist markdown formatting characters

Build backend/agents/orchestrator/orchestrator_agent.py using Temporal for reliable multi-agent coordination.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define Temporal workflow: ScrapeAndRankWorkflow
- Activities: scrape_all_sources, rank_jobs, personalise_results, notify_user
- Retry policy: exponential backoff base=1s, multiplier=2, max=60s, jitter=uniform(0, 1)
- Dead-letter queue: failed activities moved to DLQ after 3 consecutive failures
- Idempotency: SHA-256 of job URL used as workflow ID (prevents duplicate processing)
- Temporal worker process separate from uvicorn (Supervisor program:temporal-worker)
- Checkpoint after each activity — worker restart resumes from last checkpoint

EFFORT: XL (12 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Orchestration
DEPENDENCIES: JD-44, JD-45

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Temporal workflow starts and completes end-to-end
- Retry logic fires on activity failure
- DLQ receives failed activities after 3 failures
- Duplicate job URLs not processed twice

EDGE CASES:
- Temporal server unavailable: queue locally and retry on reconnect
- Workflow timeout: set 24h maximum execution time

Implement per-agent circuit breakers with configurable failure thresholds and backoff.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Use tenacity library for circuit breaker pattern
- Per-agent breaker: opens after 3 consecutive failures
- Open state: immediately return CircuitOpenError without calling agent
- Half-open state: allow 1 probe request after backoff expires
- Backoff: base=1s, exponential, max=60s
- Log state transitions (closed/open/half-open) as structured JSON
- Wrap each agent.run() call in circuit breaker in orchestrator

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Reliability
DEPENDENCIES: JD-50

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Breaker opens after 3 consecutive agent failures
- Open breaker returns error immediately without calling agent
- Breaker resets after successful probe request
- State transitions logged

EDGE CASES:
- Different timeout thresholds per agent (scraper vs ranking vs RAG)
- Breaker state must not persist across server restarts (in-memory per process)

Create GET /api/v1/admin/dlq, retry, and discard endpoints plus replay_dlq.py admin script.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- GET /api/v1/admin/dlq: list all failed workflow items with error details and retry count
- POST /api/v1/admin/dlq/{id}/retry: requeue item for processing
- DELETE /api/v1/admin/dlq/{id}: discard item permanently (with audit log entry)
- All admin routes require Admin JWT claim (RBAC via security agent)
- backend/admin/replay_dlq.py: CLI script: uv run python admin/replay_dlq.py --id {id}
- DLQ stored in Redis (MVP 2 backing service)

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Admin
DEPENDENCIES: JD-50, JD-49

TESTING CRITERIA:
- GET /api/v1/admin/dlq returns list of failed items
- Retry moves item back to processing queue
- Discard removes item and creates audit log
- Non-admin requests rejected with 403

EDGE CASES:
- Empty DLQ: return empty list (not 404)
- Retry of already-processing item: return 409 Conflict
